claims.text: A secret cabal — synonymous with the Illuminati — is using a long-running NASA-and-UN-led program called Project Blue Beam to dissolve borders, dismantle religions, and impose a New World Order under their control.

evidence.quote:
your world is not as it seems everything
you know to be true is a lie hidden
behind the facade of our everyday lives
is a secret and Powerful organization
with a dangerous agenda to manipulate
the minds of the masses and Usher in

claims.rationale: This is the umbrella thesis of the Project Blue Beam conspiracy theory, originally promoted by Serge Monast in his 1994 book. There is no evidence NASA or the UN run such a program; both are well-monitored public institutions, and the claim assumes capacities (global hologram broadcast, mass mind control via satellites) that do not exist. The host walks back the totalizing version in the verdict.

claims.text: Quebec investigative journalist Serge Monast (born 1945) was the first to expose Project Blue Beam, publishing the original 1994 book of that name based on his investigations into a secret elite cabal.

evidence.quote:
was a mysterious and controversial
figure born in Quebec in 1945 he was an
investigative journalist who became
weary of the direction in which society
was headed he believed that there was a
secret cabal of powerful Elites who were
controll

claims.rationale: Serge Monast was a real Québécois journalist (1945–1996) who published 'Project Blue Beam (NASA)' in 1994. The biographical core is true. Whether his investigations actually 'exposed' a real program — vs. synthesized one from open speculation — is the contested part addressed in later claims. The host is reporting the biographical scaffolding accurately.

claims.text: Project Blue Beam was conceived during the Cold War, when the U.S. and Soviet Union were privately experimenting with mind control and psychic warfare alongside their public arms race — making it a continuation of programs like the CIA's MK-Ultra (started 1953), but using technology rather than drugs.

evidence.quote:
project bluebeam was
conceived during the Cold War the United
States and the Soviet Union were
spending vast amounts of money and
resources on technology to gain the
upper hand no idea was off the table now
publicly the superpowers were building
tanks and plan

claims.rationale: MK-Ultra is real and the 1953 founding date is correct. Cold War mind-control research did happen on both sides. However, the claim that 'Project Blue Beam was conceived during the Cold War' as a coherent multi-agency program presupposes Blue Beam exists as more than Monast's writing, which the verdict section largely retracts. So the framing chains real history to an unsupported continuation.

claims.text: Step one of Project Blue Beam: artificial earthquakes triggered at strategic sites worldwide will surface staged 'archaeological discoveries' that discredit every existing religion's doctrines, creating a crisis of faith that softens believers up for the new one-world religion.

evidence.quote:
first stage of the project we'll see a
breakdown of all archaeological
knowledge this is done by creating
artificial earthquakes these earthquakes
would be at strategically placed
locations around the globe at these
locations new discoveries will be made
that

claims.rationale: The host explicitly debunks this in the verdict: 'earthquakes can be created but they can't be created discreetly — you need large teams of people and huge equipment.' Induced seismicity from injection wells exists but is small-magnitude and untargetable to specific archaeological sites. The 'forge artifacts via earthquake' premise has no plausible operational pathway.

claims.text: Step two — the 'space show' — uses a satellite network with lasers to project massive 3D holographic deities tailored to each region's dominant religion (Jesus, Muhammad, Buddha, Krishna), then merges them into one figure who reveals himself as the Antichrist of the new one-world faith.

evidence.quote:
space show 3D holographic images and
sounds will be projected from a network
of satellites these satellites will use
lasers to project images down to earth
these images will be designed to seduce
people into believing in a new God
Holograms and augme

then the projections of Jesus Muhammad
Buddha and Krishna will merge into one
and explanations of mysteries and
Revelations will be disclosed this one
God will in fact be the Antichrist who
will explain that the various scriptures
had been

claims.rationale: The host directly debunks this: 'A hologram is an object that can be seen floating in space by an observer without a camera — but Holograms need a substrate or a medium.' Sky-projected, sourceless holograms at planetary scale are physically impossible — light needs to scatter off particulate, and even good Pepper's Ghost / volumetric displays require carefully controlled angles and substrates. The technology to produce these effects sky-wide does not exist.

claims.text: Step three uses ELF/VLF/LF microwave-band radio waves transmitted from satellites to penetrate every human skull and inject artificial 'thoughts' indistinguishable from one's own conscience, weaponizing the ambient electromagnetic environment we already swim in.

evidence.quote:
step three is mind control using
microwaves so they put secret messages
in popcorn and Frozen Guinness no not
microwave ovens microwaves are small
radio waves oh these electromagnetic
waves are all around us all the time
they're in the air

claims.rationale: Specific mind-influence effects from ELF/VLF have been investigated (e.g., Frey microwave auditory effect), but these are limited in range, require coherent signals, and cannot inject articulate semantic content into thoughts. There is no demonstrated technology that can broadcast directed semantic content to specific individuals globally via radio. Adjacent real tech (TMS, ECS, BCIs that the host mentions next) operates only with direct hardware contact and at small scale.

claims.text: Step four — the most insidious — stages a fake alien invasion sufficiently terrifying that nuclear-armed nations exhaust their arsenals trying to fight it, after which the UN reveals the deception and forces global disarmament; the recent rash of unidentified-object shootdowns over the U.S. may be the rollout already underway.

evidence.quote:
step four is the most
Insidious of all it describes how
project bluebeam will create a fake
disaster or crisis that will bring the
world together but not through hope or
through peace the world will come
together for fear this event will be so
frightening that

Serge benass said the fourth step in
project bluebeam would be what he called
a universal Supernatural manifestation
with electronic means in other words a
false flag attack one is to make mankind
believe that an alien invasion is about
to occur at every major

claims.rationale: The 'fake alien invasion → forced disarmament' chain is internally incoherent: nation-states with nuclear arsenals are not in fact going to expend them on unidentified objects without reverse-engineering capture attempts, and the UN has no enforcement mechanism to compel post-fact disarmament. The 2023 unidentified-object shootdowns the host references were almost certainly civilian/research balloons (NORAD increased radar-filter sensitivity after the Chinese balloon incident).

claims.text: Real-world false flag operations do happen — Operation Northwoods (1962) was a U.S. Joint Chiefs proposal to attack U.S. civilians and blame Cuba (Kennedy rejected it), and the Gulf of Tonkin's second 1964 attack on the USS Maddox never occurred but was used to escalate the Vietnam War — establishing a documented precedent for the kind of deception Project Blue Beam alleges.

evidence.quote:
operation Northwoods the plan
and called for the shooting down of the
U.S civilian aircraft attacks on U.S
bases and the staging of attacks on U.S
cities all to create the illusion that
America was being attacked by Cuba
President Kennedy rejected the idea but

Gulf of
Tonkin off the coast of Vietnam the U.S
immediately escalated its involvement in
the Vietnam War but the second attack on
the Maddox it never happened the United
States would see 50 000 soldiers killed
and over 300 000 wounded in a losing War
based par

claims.rationale: Operation Northwoods is fully documented (declassified Joint Chiefs memo, March 1962). The second Gulf of Tonkin incident has been definitively shown to not have occurred — confirmed by NSA historian Robert Hanyok's 2005 declassified study of intercepts. The casualty figures are roughly correct (~58,000 U.S. KIA, ~150,000 wounded, though the 300,000 figure is closer to the wounded-in-action plus injured tally). This is the strongest historical anchor in the video.

claims.text: Monast's children were taken into state wardship in September 1996 to receive government public education, and on December 4th 1996 he was arrested for spreading misinformation and the next day found dead in his apartment of an alleged heart attack at age 51 — a suspiciously timed silencing of a whistleblower.

evidence.quote:
September 1996 his two homeschooled
children were taken from him and made
Wards of the state so they could receive
a public education run by the government
yep he never saw them again on December
4th 1996 Serge menast was arrested for
sprea

claims.rationale: Monast did die December 5, 1996 of a reported heart attack at 51, the day after being held overnight by police on an unrelated matter (his wife/family/movement maintain he was 'silenced'; published Quebec coroner accounts attribute natural causes). The custody-of-children claim is repeated in conspiracy literature but is not well sourced. Mid-range directTruth: the death date and circumstances are real, but the causal 'silenced' framing is interpretive.

claims.text: Project Blue Beam is most likely fiction reverse-engineered from Star Trek: a Gene Roddenberry biography released months before Monast's 1994 book contained the unmade Roddenberry script 'The God Thing' (alien posing as God via tech), and the 1991 TNG episode 'Devil's Due' uses holograms and mind control to do exactly what Monast describes — published 18 months before his book.

evidence.quote:
the God thing the plot of the movie was
how an alien convinces everyone on Earth
that it's a god using technology in 1991
an episode of stars Trek the Next
Generation was released called Devil's
Due the plot was about an alien using
technology to convince a pl

claims.rationale: Both works exist as the host describes: 'The God Thing' is a real Roddenberry script (later novelized by Michael Jan Friedman in 2002, but the manuscript circulated in early-90s biographies including David Alexander's 1994 'Star Trek Creator'). 'Devil's Due' is TNG S4E13, aired Feb 1991. The claim of plot-template borrowing is plausible and is the cleanest deflationary explanation for where Monast's specific imagery came from. Probably true as a chain-of-influence claim.

dependencies.rationale: If the four-step plan is downstream of Star Trek scripts, it isn't a real institutional program.

claims.text: Even if the elites wanted to execute Project Blue Beam, they can't — a worldwide hologram-and-mind-control conspiracy requires armies of carpenters, electricians, network engineers and frontline soldiers willing to turn against their families and neighbors, which the host argues simply will not happen at scale.

evidence.quote:
you couldn't
pull off a worldwide conspiracy like
this without an army of people
supporting it an army of Regular People
Like Us just think of the technical
expertise required the number of
computer Engineers Carpenters
electricians here's what I want you to
d

claims.rationale: This is a sound general epistemic point about large conspiracies: secret-keeping decays exponentially with the number of participants (David Grimes 2016 PLOS ONE estimated upper bounds on conspiracy survival vs. participant count). The host's specific craft-knowledge example is rhetorically apt. The argument is persuasive though not dispositive — 'too many participants' has been an under-rated check on grand-cabal scenarios.

dependencies.rationale: Generalized 'too many participants required' argument against the umbrella thesis.

claims.text: Government attempts to abolish religion — Roman persecution of Christians, the Soviet Union's anti-religious campaign, and Mao-era China's suppression of worship and execution of religious leaders — have failed every time across history; therefore the religious-eradication step of Project Blue Beam would also fail.

evidence.quote:
but is it true well there's no way to
fully debunk a theory like this because
anything happening behind the scenes is
presumably still behind the scenes but
let me give you the complete picture so
you can make up your own mind according
to manast Step One is i

claims.rationale: Historical record supports this: Roman persecution did not extinguish Christianity (which became Rome's state religion under Constantine); Soviet anti-religious campaigns (1920s–80s) saw the Russian Orthodox Church survive and rebound; Cultural Revolution destruction of Buddhist/Christian sites in China was followed by partial restoration. The general inference — that institutional religion is resilient against state suppression — has good empirical backing.

claims.text: Sky-projection 'holograms' as imagined in Project Blue Beam don't physically exist: a true hologram requires a physical medium or substrate to scatter the light, and the technology to make a region-wide convincing free-space projection of a deity simply isn't available with current physics, only AR through devices and Pepper's-ghost stage tricks.

evidence.quote:
the
technology to make that kind of hologram
convincing enough just doesn't exist now
for a few years there have been videos
floating around the internet showing
very realistic Holograms as a way to
support the existence of project
bluebeam none of these are r

claims.rationale: Physically correct: light only becomes visible when it scatters off something, so free-space holograms require either particulate media (fog, smoke), volumetric laser ionization in air (small-scale lab demos only, e.g., Aerial Burton's millimeter-scale plasma displays), or screen substrates. Region-wide deity projections in clear sky are not feasible with known physics.
